avijjāgatā.meaning: gone with ignorance; fallen into ignorance
breakdown: Ignorance; not knowing reality as it is (a- “not” + vijjā “knowledge”).
Gone, gone into; past participle of gacchati.

avijjānusayasamugghātakaṁ.meaning: abolishing the latent tendency of ignorance
avijjānusayasamugghātakaṁ.summary: This compound describes what uproots the deepest layer of ignorance: latent + ignorance + abolishing.
breakdown: Latent tendency; impurity lying dormant below the surface of the mind.

avinipātadhammo.meaning: not liable to fall into the lower realms of suffering
avinipātadhammo.gram: adjective, nominative singular masculine, compound (na + vinipāta + dhamma)
breakdown: The lower realms of suffering; downfall.
Having the nature of, liable to (as final member of a compound).

avinipātadhammā.meaning: not liable by nature to the lower fields of existence
breakdown: The lower fields of existence; downfall.
Having the nature of, liable to (as final member of a compound).

avippaṭisāratthāni.meaning: having freedom from regret as their purpose
breakdown: Regret, remorse (vi + paṭi + √sar, with sandhi doubling of the p).

avippaṭisārānisaṁsānī.meaning: having freedom from regret as their advantage

aviruddho.meaning: not opposed; not contradictory; not hostile
aviruddho.gram: past participle, nominative singular masculine, of na virundhati (na + vi + √rudh)
breakdown: Opposed, hostile, contradictory; past participle of virundhati.

avirūḷhichandā.meaning: with cravings whose growth has ceased; in whom desire grows no more

avisaggatā.meaning: the state of being undisturbed; harmony, balance

breakdown: Deception, breaking one’s word; from vi + saṁ + √vad.

avisaṁvādanatāya.gram: feminine abstract noun, instrumental singular (na + visaṁvādanā + -tā)
breakdown: Abstract suffix -tā in the instrumental singular.
Applied thought, initial application of the mind; from vi + √takk.

avivadamānā.meaning: not disputing; in harmony, in agreement
avivadamānā.gram: present participle, plural, of na vivadati (na + vi + √vad)
breakdown: Disputing, quarrelling; present participle (middle voice) of vivadati.

aviññātāresu.meaning: amongst the unlearned; among those who do not understand
aviññātāresu.gram: masculine noun, locative plural of aviññātar (na + vi + √ñā + tar)
breakdown: One who understands; agent noun from vijānāti.

avāgata.note: uncertain: the lexicon gives only the contextual gloss “are fallen from”; the parse as ava + āgata is inferred.

avītikkamanādhippāyena.meaning: with the intention of not transgressing
avītikkamanādhippāyena.gram: masculine noun, instrumental singular, compound (a + vītikkamana + adhippāya)
breakdown: With intention; instrumental singular of adhippāya (the vowels coalesce to -nādhi-).

ayamanusiṭṭhi.meaning: this is the instruction; this is the advice given
ayamanusiṭṭhi.gram: sandhi phrase: ayaṁ + anusiṭṭhi (from anusāsati “to instruct”)
breakdown: This (the m surfaces before the following vowel).
Instruction, advice; related to the past participle of anusāsati.

ayaṁ ekabījī.meaning: he is a “one-seeder” (a stream-enterer with only one more birth remaining)
breakdown: Having one seed (eka “one” + bīja “seed” + -ī); a sotāpanna who will take only one more human birth.

ayaṁ kolaṁkolo.meaning: he is one who goes from clan to clan (a stream-enterer reborn two or three more times in good families)
breakdown: From clan to clan (kula “clan” reduplicated); a sotāpanna with a few favourable rebirths remaining.

ayaṁ sattakkhattuparamo.meaning: he is a “seven times at the most” (a stream-enterer with at most seven more births)
breakdown: Seven times (sattakkhattuṁ) at the most (parama); a sotāpanna who will be reborn at most seven more times.

ayogakkhemakāmā.meaning: not desiring (another’s) security; wishers of one’s insecurity
breakdown: Safety, security; freedom from the bonds (yoga).

ayoniso.meaning: unwisely; erratically, unmethodically; without proper attention
ayoniso.gram: indeclinable adverb (na + yoniso “down to the origin”)

ayyirakena.meaning: by the nobleman; by the master
ayyirakena.gram: masculine noun, instrumental singular of ayyiraka (diminutive of ariya)
breakdown: Master, nobleman; a contracted form of ariya “noble”.

ayyo.gram: masculine noun, nominative/vocative singular (contracted from ariya “noble”)

añjali-karaṇīyo.meaning: worthy of reverence with folded hands
breakdown: The gesture of folded hands raised in respect.
To be done, worthy of being made; potential participle of karoti.

añjalikaraṇīyā.meaning: worthy of respect shown by raising folded hands
añjalikaraṇīyā.summary: An epithet of the Saṅgha in the recollection of its qualities: worthy of the añjali salutation.
añjalikaraṇīyā.gram: potential participle of añjaliṁ karoti, plural, compound
breakdown: Folded hands raised in respectful salutation.
To be done, worthy of being made; potential participle of karoti.

aññadatthuharo.meaning: taking everything; one who seizes all (a false friend who only takes)
aññadatthuharo.gram: adjective, nominative singular masculine, compound (aññadatthu + hara)
breakdown: Certainly, in any case; here: whatever there is, everything.
The other (añña reduplicated with bridging m): “one another”.
One another, each other (with bridging m before the following vowel).

aññamaññānulomatā.meaning: mutual suitability; being fit for one another, in natural order
breakdown: Suitable, fitting, in natural order (literally “with the grain”; the vowels coalesce to -aññānu-).

aññataradvārappavattā.meaning: proceeding through one or another door (of action: body, speech or mind)
breakdown: Proceeding, resulting; from pa + √vatt (with sandhi doubling of the p).

aññataraññatarena.meaning: by one or another; by this or that
breakdown: By another similar one; the two words coalesce in sandhi.

aññatitthiyā.meaning: adherents of other sects; followers of other beliefs
breakdown: Sectarians, followers of (other) teachers; from tittha “ford, sect”.

aññācariyavādātu.meaning: of the teachings of other teachers

aññāsi.gram: verb, aorist 3rd person singular of ājānāti (ā + √ñā)

aññātamānino.meaning: proud of being one who knows; conceited about (supposed) knowledge
breakdown: Known, understood; past participle of ājānāti.
Being proud of, conceiving oneself as; from māna “conceit”.

aññū.meaning: having knowledge, knowing (as final member of compounds such as kataññū, varaññū)

aḍayhamānena.meaning: not burning; not being consumed or tormented
aḍayhamānena.gram: present participle, instrumental singular, of na ḍayhati (passive of ḍahati “to burn”)
breakdown: Burning, being consumed; present participle (passive) of ḍahati, instrumental singular.

aḍḍhateyyavassasatāyukānaṁ.meaning: of those whose lifespan is two and a half centuries
aḍḍhateyyavassasatāyukānaṁ.gram: adjective, genitive plural, compound (aḍḍhateyya + vassasata + āyuka)
breakdown: Two and a half (literally “half of the third”).
Having a lifespan; genitive plural (sata + āyuka coalesces to satāyuka).

aḍḍhe.gram: adjective, locative singular or accusative plural of aḍḍha

aṅguttara.meaning: increasing by one factor; “by one limb higher” (as in the Aṅguttara Nikāya, whose discourses are arranged by ascending numbers)
breakdown: Higher, above, further (aṅga + uttara coalesces to aṅguttara).
